plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-261 RP07.P07.M02.S13-RP07.P07.M04.S05 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-261 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP07.P07.M02 type/shape tail (rollback expectation through human escalation note), the full M03 primary failure cycle (25 rows including no-silent-success and no-data-leak checks), and the M04 secondary failure head (5 rows) across three sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence, Claude review packet, and CP00-262 handoff; the shared failure-row guards are validated across every section, without opening lock acquisition, retry/rollback runtime, integration smoke dispatch, AI runtime dispatch, search/OCR/index/embedding runtime, state writes, object storage, command runtime execution, Hermes runtime receipts, email, Citation Ledger, or Loop behavior.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-261 is a clean, descriptor-only failure-slice pack (RP07.P07.M02.S13–RP07.P07.M04.S05, risk B, 40 units) consistent with the CP00-235..CP00-260 lineage. The pack-level production_ready claim is supported: status is implemented_pending_review with production_ready:false (the expected pre-closeout state), while the Hermes evidence packet reports production_ready_candidate:true (coverage.valid && slice.valid). Independent inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms: (1) the CP261 binding (registry.js:469), requirements (registry.js:2374), contract current_pack + failure_slice_descriptor projection, closeout-pack-plan, and manifest all agree on range, risk class, unit_count (40), upstream CP00-260 and next CP00-262, and the descriptor name SearchCoreCp261FailureSliceDescriptor; (2) hand-recomputed deliverable distribution (failure_recovery 18 / implementation 7 / hermes_evidence 4 / fixture 2 / test 4 / security_audit 3 / claude_review 2 = 40) and micro-title counts (10/25/5) match the baseline exactly, and the range is contiguous with CP260 (which ended at M02.S12); (3) all 7 required public exports exist, are auto-re-exported through index.js `export *`, and are exercised by tests sourced from the real contract and closeout plan; (4) the failure-row guards in validators.js (cross-tenant, lock, retry, rollback, blocked-claim, Hermes-receipt, plus M03 no-silent-success / no-data-leak / human-escalation / no-Claude-final-approval) are genuinely enforced — searchCoreRowKey (service.js:86) deterministically yields the exact literal keys the guards and row-extras rely on, so no guard silently short-circuits; (5) authority boundaries hold (claude_is_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required:true, no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation), and Hermes patterns are confined to descriptor-only review packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false throughout); (6) CP178+ review-hardening blockers (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, etc.) are encoded and the contract validator deep-equals the embedded descriptor/requirements/attestation. The readiness report shows a passing auth preflight (valid JSON, 1445B stdout), the validation report confirms read-only tools and a correctly-prohibited summary-only mode, and command-evidence records all implementation/contract/plan/repo-wide validation commands at exit 0. No real client/matter/document/credential data and no product-state writes are involved. One non-blocking P3 note on scope-artifact labeling. No P0/P1/P2 issues; pack is ready for human-gated closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope lists the pack's own manifest dir under both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-261/ (the pack's own manifest + command-evidence) and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (lines 95-98) and in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 100-105). The cp00-261/ directory is genuinely part of this pack's evidence, so categorizing it as 'unrelated' is slightly contradictory. Net effect is benign: active_pack_required_untracked_files and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted are both empty, the 8 tracked implementation refs are correctly identified, and the manifest evidence is present and was read during review. This is a labeling nuance in the review-tooling scope artifact, not a defect in the pack's source, tests, contract, or validation.
findings.recommendation: Optionally refine the scope generator so a pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<id>/ directory is classified solely as pack-owned evidence rather than also appearing under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, to avoid reader confusion. No action required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking informational; does not affect pack production_ready claim or closeout. Tracked for tooling clarity only.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-261 search core failure slice descriptor pack
